constraints: Given a parameter MMM, the score of an array A=[A1,A2,...,AN]A = [A_1, A_2, ..., A_N]A=[A1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,...,AN‚Äã] can be computed as follows :
Construct a new array B=[B1,B2,....,BN]B = [B_1, B_2, ...., B_N]B=[B1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,....,BN‚Äã] where BiB_iBi‚Äã is found as follows :
Let SiS_iSi‚Äã be a set containing all Aj(1‚â§j‚â§N)A_j (1 \le j \le N)Aj‚Äã(1‚â§j‚â§N) values such that Aj‚â§AiA_j \le A_iAj‚Äã‚â§Ai‚Äã.
BiB_iBi‚Äã is the size of the set SiS_iSi‚Äã

Informally, BBB is just the "coordinate-compressed" array form of AAA. For example, for A=[4,2,4,7,7,5]A = [4, 2, 4, 7, 7, 5]A=[4,2,4,7,7,5], the corresponding array B=[2,1,2,4,4,3]B = [2, 1, 2, 4, 4, 3]B=[2,1,2,4,4,3].
Find the sum of scores of all arrays of size NNN satisfying 1‚â§Ai‚â§K1 \le A_i \le K1‚â§Ai‚Äã‚â§K. Since the answer may be large, output it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
The first and only line of each test case contains 333 integers, NNN, MMM and KKK.
For each test case, output on a new line the sum of scores of all arrays of size NNN satisfying 1‚â§Ai‚â§K1 \le A_i \le K1‚â§Ai‚Äã‚â§K mod 998244353998244353998244353.
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : There are 444 arrays, [1][1][1], [2][2][2], [3][3][3], and [4][4][4]. All of them get compressed to [1][1][1] and hence their scores are all 111. Thus, the sum is 444
Test Case 2 : There are 888 total arrays. Here are 222 of them and their score :

[1,2,1][1, 2, 1][1,2,1] : remains same after compression, score is 11+21+11=41^1 + 2^1 + 1^1 = 411+21+11=4
[2,2,2][2, 2, 2][2,2,2] : becomes [1,1,1][1, 1, 1][1,1,1] after compression, score is 11+11+11=31^1 + 1^1 + 1^1 = 311+11+11=3.
